author: codex
scope: Codex-only relation endpoint-shape rule-or-data triage; read-only deterministic inspection plus this single prep artifact; no generated-output, provider-backed, durable data, or code mutations

recent_batches_not_repeated: relation cleanup batches037-040 for timeline/published_by/implemented_by buckets
current_checkpoint_context.inspection_method: Read-only SQLite queries against data/canonical.db plus source block JSONL inspection; endpoint table transcribed from packages/pipeline/src/records/relations.ts after confirming current tests accept owned_by and operated_by corridor->entity.

remaining_bucket_counts.triage_note: All current exact endpoint-shape buckets are singletons, so selection favors a clean reusable ontology rule with narrow blast radius.

recommended_patch.code_direction: Add treatment_component to RELATION_ENDPOINT_SHAPES.has_claim.subject and add a focused relationEndpointShapeIssue test that accepts has_claim treatment_component->claim while keeping claim->claim invalid.
recommended_patch.why_semantically_valid: Treatment components can carry source-stated attribute or condition claims, such as operating hours, eligibility, restrictions, or stated benefits. The selected row links a bus-lane treatment component to a claim that states its operating hours; this preserves a useful treatment-specific assertion without inventing a project, route, or corridor endpoint.
recommended_patch.why_not_data_cleanup: The source already has accepted project->treatment and corridor->treatment context for the Kings Highway bus lanes. Replacing the selected row with project->claim or corridor->claim would make the hours claim less specific, while retire-only would discard a source-backed treatment attribute. The current relation kind and direction are the most precise shape if treatment_component subjects are admitted for has_claim.
recommended_patch.why_not_broader_rule_change: This does not justify allowing treatment_component subjects for has_treatment, implemented_by, operated_by, owned_by, published_by, or timeline relations. It also does not justify claim subjects for has_claim.

object.display_name: Bus Lane Hours on Kings Highway

payload_summary.description: The proposed bus lanes have operating hours of 7 AM - 7 PM all days

evidence.quote: Red line = Bus Lane (7 AM - 7 PM all days)
Install bus lanes along 8 block faces
Bus Lane (7 AM - 7 PM all days)

related_valid_context.triage_read: Already valid project context for the same bus-lane treatment; it does not replace treatment-specific operating-hours claim context.
Already valid corridor context for the same treatment; it should remain separate from the proposed treatment->claim rule.

representative_records.triage_read: Clean source-backed treatment attribute claim; best handled by a scoped validator rule patch.

evidence.quote: The map shows 23 stations completed since 2020 and 6 stations to be completed by the end of the year.

representative_records.triage_read: Counterexample for a broader has_claim subject expansion. Claim->claim conflates nested assertions/list extraction and should remain invalid for data cleanup or possible claim modeling refinement.

evidence.quote: Welcome back to a new elevator!

representative_records.triage_read: Counterexample for widening treatment rules generally. This row asks whether a claim can own a treatment component, which is a different data-cleanup/modeling problem from treatment_component carrying a claim.

evidence.quote: we activate enforcement on the Bx36 route

representative_records.triage_read: Plausible later rule-or-data question for route-level treatments, but it has broader modeling implications and should not be bundled with this has_claim treatment_component->claim patch.

source_and_canonical_evidence_summary: The selected relation, treatment component, and claim are all from brt_kings_highway_aug2018 and use the same local observation ids supplied by the ingest submission.
Source block p001_c0004 states the bus-lane legend and operating hours; p001_c0007 states the bus-lane installation scope; p002_c0004 repeats the bus-lane hours in the project legend.

Current valid neighboring relations already connect the project and corridor to the treatment component, so the remaining issue is not a missing project/corridor carrier but a treatment-specific claim-context rule.

current_shape_inventory_for_selected_fam.interpretation: The proposed patch admits only the treatment_component->claim singleton. The claim->claim singleton remains outside the accepted subject set.

non_goals: Do not retire, replace, or edit canonical data as part of this prep.
Do not apply the code patch in this artifact-only lane.
Do not widen has_claim to claim->claim, metric_claim->claim, table->claim, source_gap->claim, or relation->claim.
Do not widen has_treatment, implemented_by, operated_by, owned_by, published_by, has_timeline_event, related_route, serves_route, or operates_on_corridor based on this evidence.
Do not reinterpret the Kings Highway treatment as a project, corridor, or route merely to satisfy the current validator.
Do not repeat the already-handled owned_by/operated_by corridor->entity patches or relation cleanup batches037-040.

commands_run_read_only: bun --eval '<read-only sqlite endpoint-shape inventory using current RELATION_ENDPOINT_SHAPES table>'
sqlite3 -readonly data/canonical.db '<selected records and related context queries>'
rg -n '<selected submission ids/local ids>' data/submissions data/review_notes -S
commands_intentionally_not_run: any command with --profile or provider-backed execution
